item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gather that this has something to do with 2845 but that's about as far as I can figure out.

+ random thoughts
- begone, random thoughts

Given the (needlessly?) esoteric method for entering 5845 I have to wonder how the Foundation even discovered it in the first place. Are they just shooting people into random celestial bodies and seeing what happens?

"thaumonuclear explosive" is amazing, but I feel like using 'gigaton' is a missed opportunity for a fun "magick" term

The 1km sphere completely baffles me. Was it the containment cell? Is the 'square pyramidal hole' a spot for one of the frequently mentioned nails? Does the lack of anything in the hole mean that containment has failed and whatever was within has escaped and if so why aren't there any signs of something escaping?

I really enjoy the containment consultant's document but the physical document is left too vague for me. Without a physical description of the document there's no clue as to when or how it was written. Is it written with ink on parchment? Typed up on an modern sheet of 8.5x11 paper? And what language is it in? Renaissance Latin? Italian? English? I feel like these kind of details could help give us more clues as to what is going on here. Ditto for the writing on the silver plaque.

The murals/reliefs have some incredible imagery in them, they're probably my favorite part of the article. No idea what they mean, but they're very evocative.

Overall I think it's very well written but the puzzle pieces are just too vague to make a cohesive whole for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was about Able or the Chaos Insurgency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no clue what's going on here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I'm sure this all makes sense to someone. Goodness knows I like coming up with my own convoluted personal symbolic systems.

I think your need to make specific details clear is exceeded by your medium. The cabin being made of olive wood, the crown of specifically eagle feathers, the figure holding unleavened bread…these must be extremely detailed reliefs if these things can be determined by sonar imaging of a carving. Btw, it does seem that what you are describing are reliefs (shallow carved imagery on a wall or surface) rather than murals (2D, pigmented imagery on wall, usually painted).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Round peg, square hole. See Roget's Proposal, expanded on in SCP-2998 and re-used in SCP-5000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you give me a clue? I feel like I should know what that is, but it’s eluding me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The clear passage allows contact with the sphere where there is a single, square pyramidal hole 2.5 cm in width and 15 cm in depth.

When I figured out what this was referencing, I felt that familiar dread encroaching on the read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can definitely tell this was written with care and purpose; a lot of the elements seem random but I get the feeling they're not. Hit-or-Miss: most DoA articles are a 'miss' for me, though this is definitely one of the better ones in my opinion. That said, I'm novoting until I can figure out what the two answers are that the author mentioned and what this is a reimagining of, though I have some ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Department of Abnormalities skips aren't very blatant and that mysteriousness is part of their charm which I have had some trouble balancing with understanding during the drafting of this. I eventually settled on wanting the reader to walk away from this article understanding two things about the DoA's actions here, what this place contained and what they are trying to hide. Those two questions don't have the same answer but they shared a history.

Per other DoA articles, this often requires some knowledge of other SCPs so popular ones are often selected by the author. What this place is built for isn't as well known as some, like how SCP-3220 re-imagines 173s containment or SCP-5832 with the Scarlet King, but chances are you have probably heard of this one. I only hope I'm pulling this off with even half the ability as those authors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed the first part of this SCP and the second part was…interesting, i like the connection to the Department of Abnormalities as i only recently found out about them and love the concept. As for the rest, the imagery was interesting for sure but maybe due to a lack of almost any context whether my own or due to the article i didn't feel one way or another about them.

I think with a slight addition, providing some context to this hyper religious…whatever this ship was, i'd like it a lot more.
No vote for now but i'd very much like to give it a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't confidently vote without knowing at least an iota of what the heck is going on :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor edit request: In the Containment Procedures you have "Entry into SCP-5845requires", you may want to change that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was weird, very complicated, and an ultimately satisfying read. I really enjoy complex, ultra-religious kinds of esoterica, and this hit just the right tone with me.

+1, easily.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
Image author is NASA/Goddard Space Flight Center Science Visualization Studio/Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory/Carnegie Institution of Washington under the public domain license.
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gravity_Anomalies_on_Mercury.jpg

Thanks to the critters for this.
MalyceGraves, Oboebandgeek99, Stormbreath, cybersqyd, (ultra early draft that this doesn't at all look like anymore first draft look half a year ago) Rounderhouse and Absentmindednihilist.
